title: melica papilionacea var. hyalina
intro: Melica papilionacea var. hyalina, also known as blue melic grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of blue melic grass include its ability to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Blue melic grass can be prepared as a tea or tincture for use in herbal remedies, or used topically as a poultice to treat skin irritations and wounds.
